pi_items.source: PySAM execution with allowed SAM TMY weather files (e.g. Fargo, ND) yields ~29.1M kWh, which falls below this claimed minimum.
PySAM execution with allowed high-insolation sites yields ~43M kWh, safely within this max bound.
PySAM execution with allowed low-insolation sites yields ~1783 kWh/m2, safely within this min bound.
PySAM execution with allowed SAM TMY weather files (e.g. Blythe, CA, Imperial, CA) yields insolation > 2850 kWh/m2, contradicting this claimed maximum.
PySAM execution confirms PR values across tested sites (0.73 - 0.81) comfortably exceed this minimum.
PySAM execution confirms PR values across tested sites (0.73 - 0.81) comfortably fall below this maximum.

privileged_info_summary: I verified the expected filename and 20000 kW capacity directly from the prompt instructions. I executed PySAM using the `pvwattsv8` module across multiple real TMY weather files found in the standard SAM `solar_resource` directory. I discovered that the claimed `expected_energy_min` (30,000,000) and `expected_insolation_max` (2800) contradict the task's instruction to use 'any available TMY weather file', as valid files like Fargo, ND yield ~29.1M kWh and Imperial, CA yields ~2866 insolation. Additionally, I discovered that the task is defective because `verifier.py` is entirely missing from the environment, making the task programmatically unscorable.
